rationale.R1.1: The artifact makes a strong contribution claim ('achieves linearizable reads and writes while tolerating f failures'); checking whether the protocol description actually warrants that claim is central to any meaningful review of this artifact.
rationale.R1.2: The protocol contains several logical pressure points — the lease mechanism is introduced without reconciling it with the two-phase exchange, leader election by 'highest sequence number' is underspecified for safety under concurrent failures, and the majority-quorum size relative to f=(N-1)/2 is asserted but not derived — making internal consistency the most load-bearing axis.
rationale.R1.3: Specific quantitative claims are made (87k ops/sec, 40% improvement, 3.1 ms median latency) with comparators; verifying whether the described experimental setup and reported figures coherently support those claims is expected scope for 'check this' applied to a performance-oriented protocol paper.

parsed.clarification_question: Should I focus primarily on the protocol's internal logical correctness (i.e., whether the lease/leader-election mechanism actually guarantees linearizability under the stated failure model), or on whether the experimental results and reported numbers validly support the stated performance claims against Raft and Multi-Paxos?
